retry_reason: Allocation or hash insertion may fail under memory pressure; retrying after memory relief may succeed.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper for a backbone gateway entry. Returns a pointer to a batadv_bla_backbone_gw with an elevated reference count. The function first attempts to find an existing entry via batadv_backbone_hash_find, which already acquires a reference for the caller. If not found, it allocates a new entry, initializes its reference count (kref_init), acquires an additional reference for the caller (kref_get), and inserts it into the hash (the hash holds a reference). Side effects for new entries include removing any global TT entry for the originator/VLAN and, if own_backbone is set, sending a loop detection announcement and transitioning to unsynced state. The caller must release the reference via batadv_bla_backbone_gw_put (or equivalent) when the entry is no longer needed. Returns NULL on memory or hash insertion failure; the caller should check the return value and may retry on transient failures.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to get-or-create a backbone gateway entry with an elevated reference count for the caller; includes significant initialization and side effects for new entries (TT deletion, announce sending) but the refcount management is central.

check.reason: The function returns a batadv_bla_backbone_gw pointer with an elevated refcount, either from batadv_backbone_hash_find (get) or from the newly created entry (kref_init + kref_get). The access path entry.refcount.refcount correctly traces from the return variable through the struct's refcount (kref) to the inner refcount_t field. The type chain matches the fields: batadv_bla_backbone_gw -> kref -> refcount_t. Segment count matches (3 segments vs 3 types).
